mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal expanding real output and stronger aggregate demand, which increases the equilibrium real interest rate and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly changes versus 20-day yield changes stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the average yield change in up-INDPRO periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8%, the market prices in a higher probability of recession and monetary easing, which compresses long-term nominal yields as safe-haven demand intensifies over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, the average 20-day yield change is positive instead of negative, or the hit rate for negative returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: An increasing ratio of retail sales (RSAFS) to housing starts (HOUST) indicates that consumption demand is outpacing residential investment, which lifts the natural rate and pushes Treasury yields higher over a 20-day horizon through expectations of tighter resource utilization.
failure_condition: A rising RSAFS/HOUST ratio does not produce a positive median 20-day yield change over a 3-year rolling period, or the correlation between the ratio change and yield change is negative and statistically significant at 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus year-over-year CPI inflation) is negative, further increases in the CPI index (CPIAUCSL) deepen negative real rates and increase inflation premium in long-term bonds, pushing the 10-year yield higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when the real rate is negative, rising CPI leads to falling yields in more than 50% of 20-day windows, or the average yield change is not significantly above zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals near-term tightening that lifts the entire yield curve through expectations of higher future short rates, causing a rise in the 10-year yield within a 5-day window.
failure_condition: The 5-day Rank IC of FEDFUNDS changes is below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or the average 5-day yield change following a FEDFUNDS increase is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) exceed a high threshold (420,000 millions of dollars), it signals exceptionally strong consumer spending that fuels inflation expectations and drives the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS is above 420,000, the average 5-day yield change is negative or the proportion of positive moves is less than 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) create a wage-price spiral narrative that drives up long-term inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL are rising month-over-month, the 60-day yield change is not positive in at least 60% of such episodes, or the average yield change is not statistically above zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening monetary cycle (identified by a sustained rise in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 months), increases in industrial production (INDPRO) are viewed as demand-pull inflationary, amplifying the yield response and leading to a rise in the 10-year yield over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months)
failure_condition: In tightening cycles, rising INDPRO does not produce a positive 60-day yield change more often than a baseline regime, or the effect is indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).
